channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Curated geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: NATO seeks clarification from US on Germany troop withdrawal
item.description: NATO is working with the United States to understand details of the planned drawdown of 5,000 US troops from Germany, a spokesperson said, as the alliance seeks clarity on implications for European security.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: US and Philippines deploy anti-ship missiles near Taiwan in war games
item.description: Philippine and US forces showcased the NMESIS anti-ship missile system in Batanes province during annual military exercises, highlighting escalating tensions in the Taiwan Strait as China claims the self-governed island.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: OPEC+ agrees to raise oil output targets despite Hormuz disruption
item.description: OPEC+ has agreed in principle to raise oil output targets in June, but the increase will remain largely theoretical as the US-Iran war continues to disrupt Gulf oil supplies through the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: Jailed Iranian Nobel laureate in critical condition after cardiac crisis
item.description: Narges Mohammadi, the 2023 Nobel Peace Prize winner imprisoned in Iran, was hospitalized after collapsing in prison with a severe cardiac condition. The Norwegian Nobel Committee called for her immediate release.
item.dc:creator: NPR

item.title: China Rejects US Sanctions on Refineries Over Iran Oil Links
item.description: Beijing's commerce ministry declared that China will not comply with US sanctions against five companies targeted for purchasing Iranian oil, escalating economic tensions between Washington and Beijing.
item.dc:creator: Al Arabiya

item.title: Hezbollah's Fiber-Optic Drones Pose Challenge for Israel
item.description: The militant group is using cheap drones linked to their launch site by thin fiber-optic cables that can stretch for dozens of kilometers, making them difficult to detect and jam.
item.dc:creator: SCMP

item.title: Mali Probes Soldiers Collaborating with Jihadist Rebels
item.description: Malian authorities announced an investigation into military officers who allegedly collaborated with jihadist and separatist fighters who carried out coordinated attacks, highlighting internal security challenges.
item.dc:creator: France 24

item.title: Former F1 Champion and Paralympic Gold Medalist Alex Zanardi Dies at 59
item.description: The Italian racing driver who lost both legs in a 2001 crash and went on to win four Paralympic gold medals in hand cycling has died after years of health complications. He became a global symbol of resilience and determination.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: Iran Standoff Could Leave Trump Worse Off Than Before He Went to War
item.description: Analysis finds the unresolved conflict is driving up US gas prices without delivering a decisive military or diplomatic win, potentially harming Trump's political prospects.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: Germany Downplays US Troop Cuts as Europe Faces New Defense Reality
item.description: Germany's defense minister says the planned withdrawal of 5,000 US troops should spur Europeans to take more responsibility for their own security, while NATO allies express concern about the timing amid Iran tensions.
item.dc:creator: AP News

item.title: Europe Rattled by 'Disastrous Trend' as Trump Pulls 5,000 Troops from Germany
item.description: European leaders call for stronger defense cooperation as the Pentagon announces troop reductions that will bring US forces in Europe back to pre-2022 levels before Russia's invasion of Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: NBC News

item.title: Pope Leo XIV Urges Wealthy US Catholics to Keep Donating
item.description: The new pontiff addressed American Catholic donors amid concerns about financial pressures on the Church and the global economic uncertainty caused by the Middle East conflict.
item.dc:creator: CP24

item.title: Imprisoned Iranian Nobel Laureate Hospitalized After Cardiac Crisis
item.description: Narges Mohammadi, the 2023 Nobel Peace Prize winner jailed in Iran, was rushed to hospital after a severe cardiac episode. The Norwegian Nobel Committee head says her life is in Iran's hands.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: Saudi Energy Minister Faces Ultimate Test After UAE's Shock OPEC Exit
item.description: Prince Abdulaziz bin Salman now faces the dual challenge of managing OPEC cohesion after the UAE's departure while navigating the largest-ever disruption to global oil supplies from the Hormuz closure.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: Iran Football Chief Barred from Canada, Chooses to Return Home
item.description: Iran's football association president said he was permitted to enter Canada but decided to return to Iran, amid tensions between Tehran and Western nations following the US-Iran conflict.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: Yemen Reports Oil Tanker Hijacked Off Shabwa Coast
item.description: Yemeni authorities say an oil tanker was seized by armed men off the country's southern coast and is heading toward Somali waters, raising concerns about maritime security in the region.
item.dc:creator: Al Arabiya

item.title: India's Logistics Pact with Russia Signals Arctic Ambitions
item.description: New Delhi's growing defense cooperation with Moscow extends to the Arctic, where India seeks to establish a strategic presence as climate change opens new shipping routes and resource opportunities.
item.dc:creator: SCMP

item.title: Taiwan Opposition Leader Eyes US Trip Amid Regional Tensions
item.description: Taiwanese opposition figure Cheng Li-wun is planning a June visit to the United States as Beijing watches closely amid heightened cross-strait tensions and the broader US-China rivalry.
item.dc:creator: SCMP

item.title: US Warns Shipping Firms on Iran 'Toll' Payments for Hormuz Transit
item.description: The State Department cautioned that companies paying Iran for safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz could face sanctions, as Tehran demands fees from vessels transiting the strategic waterway.
item.dc:creator: AP News

item.title: Two Months of Iran War Leaves Both Sides Seeking Exit
item.description: Analysis of the two-month conflict shows neither the US nor Iran has achieved decisive gains, with both sides now seeking an off-ramp amid mounting economic costs and military stalemate.
item.dc:creator: CNN

item.title: US Troop Drawdown Highlights European Defense Responsibility
item.description: Germany's defense minister says the planned withdrawal should spur Europe to strengthen its own defenses, as transatlantic relations strain under Iran war disagreements and tariff disputes.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: Trump Likens US Naval Actions Against Iran to Piracy
item.description: President Trump compared American naval operations in the Iran blockade to piracy tactics, drawing criticism and confusion about the administration's strategy in the Persian Gulf.
item.dc:creator: Daily Sabah

item.title: Israel Intercepts Gaza Flotilla, Spain Condemns 'Illegal Detention'
item.description: Israel intercepted a Gaza-bound aid vessel in international waters and brought two activists in for questioning, prompting Spain to accuse Israel of 'illegal detention' of its citizens.
item.dc:creator: France 24

item.title: Trump Declares Iran War 'Terminated' as War Powers Deadline Passes
item.description: The White House asserted that the ceasefire has 'terminated' hostilities for purposes of the congressional war powers deadline, as lawmakers debate the administration's authority to continue military operations.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: US Bypasses Congress for $8.6 Billion in Middle East Military Sales
item.description: The Trump administration approved major weapons sales to Israel, Qatar, Kuwait and the UAE without congressional review, citing national security interests amid ongoing Iran tensions.
item.dc:creator: Reuters
